question.question: Based on the detonation report, which of the following behaviors most strongly indicate that this sample is designed to evade or bypass endpoint security controls rather than perform pure data theft or encryption? (Select all that apply)
options: A. Opening the Kernel Security Device Driver (KsecDD) and interacting directly with \Device\Harddisk0\DR0, consistent with low-level bootkit-style tampering
B. Using Windows cryptographic APIs and AES primarily to encrypt user documents in place and then dropping a ransom note on disk
C. Performing extensive virtualization and sandbox checks (e.g., NtQuerySystemInformation, GetTickCount timing, vmtoolsd.exe detection) before proceeding
D. Creating or modifying Windows services via HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services to gain persistence at a privileged level
E. Leveraging process injection–related APIs (e.g., RtlCreateUserThread, VirtualAllocEx, CallNextHookEx) indicative of user-mode unhooking or stealthy code execution inside other processes
